attempt_4.result: ABORT at NEW pre-flight gap discovery — git sync axis

new_diagnostic_evidence.observation: local HEAD=289859cf, origin/main=e3e29631, local ahead by 9 commits including a4e65c6d (Axis 1 byte-weighted pool + Axis 2 p2 RoPE Qwen2.5-7B)
new_diagnostic_evidence.propagation_path: tool/h100_stage2_post_launch_chain.bash line 174: `git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/need-singularity/anima.git` → pods pull origin/main only
new_diagnostic_evidence.implication: if launched without push, pods would train with PRE-a4e65c6d code (r5 behavior) using r14 corpus. Result = r5 FAIL replay + ~$200 burn with no new evidence.
new_diagnostic_evidence.hypothesis_for_attempt_1_reinterpretatio: attempt_1 (HEAD=289859cf at time; docs note head_commit=f94d03d5 in convergence but that is actually the commit CONTAINING attempt_1 ABORT note) may also have suffered this issue — pods got origin/main at that time, trained with stale code for ~4m before spendLimit kill. The spendLimit was the PROXIMATE cause, but even without it the training would have produced r5-behavior artifacts. This means the 'fresh runway resolves mechanism' H-DIAG3 predicate does NOT cleanly apply — git sync was a hidden second axis.
new_diagnostic_evidence.resolution_required: git push origin main (user authorization required; sub-agent has no push mandate). Alternative: patch post_launch_chain.bash to rsync local tree instead of clone, but this violates anti-scope 'No additional diagnostics' and introduces new risk.

attempt_4.compliance: H-DIAG3 honored new-diagnostic-required rule — even though runway signal improved, a NEW independent failure axis was discovered before burn. Zero-cost catch.

meta_incident.pattern: 3 consecutive same-signal spendLimit ABORTs (2,3) + attempt_4 PASSES that gate with clientBalance $455 runway 8.1h, but uncovers independent git-sync axis
meta_incident.lesson_attempts_1_3: User-facing billing dashboard signal and runpodctl-queried API truth are decoupled — either account identity mismatch or propagation issue. Need a reconciliation step (user pastes runpodctl user output after dashboard action) before next launch attempt.
meta_incident.lesson_attempt_4: Pre-flight scope was incomplete — it checked API financial state and local artifact integrity but did NOT check that origin/main contains the commits encoding the experimental hypothesis. For any launch where tool/h100_stage2_post_launch_chain.bash drives pod bootstrap via `git clone origin/main`, add a pre-flight 8: `git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD` must be 0 OR the launch must first push (with user authorization).

incidents.symptom: All 4 pods disappear from `runpodctl pod list` (empty []) and `runpodctl pod get <pid>` returns HTTP 404 'pod not found' ~4 minutes after RUNNING confirmation. adapter_pull daemon stayed in 'waiting for h_last_raw.json' loop indefinitely. SSH refused on p2:18413 and p1:15690 at ~18:44:30Z.
incidents.root_cause: RunPod account spendLimit = $80 (queried via `runpodctl user` post-incident: clientBalance=$106.23, spendLimit=80). Today's cumulative H100 spend already reached $160.51 (billing pods 2026-04-24), far exceeding the $80 limit. RunPod allowed new pod creation but enforced the limit asynchronously — after ~4m of burn, the platform terminated all 4 pods without local signal.
evidence: `runpodctl billing pods` 2026-04-24 entry: $160.51 for NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 (49104940 ms billedMs = 13.6h cumulative)
Local logs show no `runpodctl remove pod` calls in /tmp/h100_stage2_launch*.log, /tmp/h100_stage2_chain*.log, /tmp/h100_adapter_pull*.log
auto_kill hexa plist is --dry-run only (no remove); runpod_credit_check.hexa has no remove-pod code path
No local launchd/cron service exists that removes pods
incidents.fix_required_before_retry: USER ACTION: raise or remove the spendLimit on RunPod account (web console → Settings → Billing → spend limit). Account email: nerve011235@gmail.com. Without this, every subsequent 4-pod launch will replicate this termination within ~4m.
incidents.prevention_code_side: Add pre-flight 7: `runpodctl user | jq '.spendLimit'` must exceed expected_burn_per_round or be 0/unlimited. If spendLimit < 250 USD, ABORT unified_launch.bash before pod creation (zero-cost check).
